sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: Synthetic **renderer stress** page for Sprint 26. Exercises Utility HTML patterns only. Not workflow-generated; not a composition exemplar.

content.statement:
This fixture exists to validate presentation rendering:
- section headings and icons
- purpose statement with inline bullets
outcomes: - Identify key renderer paths without literal list-marker leakage
1. Render tables inside strings, objects, and nested template fields
• Surface cognition and preamble blocks when upstream fields exist

concepts.name: Pipe tables
concepts.definition: Markdown tables must render as scroll-wrapped HTML tables.
Embedded '- ' or '1.' prefixes must not appear inside rendered list items.

content.relationships: Tables and lists should stay visually distinct; concepts use definition blocks, not heading stacks inside bullets.

content.summary: Answer-grid mode hides inline keys until the end section.

content.name: Generic headings
content.definition: Prefer inner titles over Text / Support Text labels.

content.title: Pattern showcase — all material types
content.purpose: Exercise every first-class material renderer path in one activity block.
content.learner_task:
Work through each material block in order.
- Complete the task cards
- Read both scenarios
- Answer every prompt
content.learner_instructions: Alternate field: numbered steps as array

task_cards.title: Card 1 — Warm-up
task_cards.text: Write one sentence defining the target skill.

task_cards.title: Card 2 — Evidence
task_cards.text: Cite one datum from the table below.

task_cards:
### Card 3 — Markdown heading card
Strip leading ### from card body when rendered.

scenarios.text: A short narrative body for **scenario A** with inline emphasis.

scenarios:
### Scenario B: Escalation path
Rent is fixed; utility costs rise 18% year-on-year.

scenarios.title: Scenario C
stages: Stage 1 — Observe: note three price changes

stages.title: Compare
stages.content: Place outcomes in the worksheet table.

prompt_set.heading: Discussion prompts
prompts: What assumption in Scenario A is weakest?
How would you explain this to a non-specialist audience in under 30 seconds?

Long prompt stress: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed non risus. Suspendisse lectus tortor, dignissim sit amet, adipiscing nec, ultricies sed, dolor. Cras elementum ultrices diam. Maecenas ligula massa, varius a, semper congue, euismod non, mi.

sections.heading: Before you submit
items: ☐ Table has at least two completed rows

escalation_template.background: Known history of asthma; no new medications.
escalation_template.recommendation: Escalate to senior nurse; prepare salbutamol per protocol.

experimental_unknown_key.heading: Unknown material shape
experimental_unknown_key.body: This key should fall through to renderMaterialValue / generic object rendering without breaking export.

content.expected_output: Completed cards, table rows, and at least two prompt responses.
content.support_note: Circulate during minutes 10–35; prompt groups to justify table ratings.

content.title: Edge cases — optional fields and fallbacks
content.learner_task: Single string learner_task only; no grouping badge expected.

materials.prompts:
## Inline markdown prompts
- First bullet prompt
- Second bullet with **bold** label

Paragraph after list.

content.expected_output: A single paragraph reflecting on trade-offs.
content.facilitator_note: Long facilitator note stress: Use this block to verify support-note wrapping, contrast, and print margins. Repeat key timing cues. Confirm groups do not skip the comparison table. Offer one worked example if table rows are empty after five minutes.

content.title: Density and print stress
content.purpose: Dense materials for spacing, print, and scannability review.
content.learner_task: Complete all tables; use checklists as you go.
content.expected_output: All tables started; at least half the cells filled.

content.title: Renderer stabilisation edge cases
content.activity_preamble: Before working through these blocks, recall how tables and lists should look on learner pages.
content.self_explanation_prompt: After each table, note one thing you had to infer from the layout.
content.transfer_or_application_task: Choose one edge case and explain how you would spot it in a live export.
scaffold_hint_sequence: Check list markers in Learning purpose above.
Finish with the comparison prompts separator test.
content.learner_task: Complete each material block; use tables and checklists as directed.

text.heading: Key Life Events
text.content: Marx was born in Trier (1818), studied in Bonn and Berlin, and later worked as a journalist in Cologne and exile capitals.

support_text.title: Class Struggle Explanation
support_text.body: Class struggle describes conflict between owners of production and workers who sell their labour.

materials.reading_text: Short orienting paragraph only — inner title should win over this generic key.
materials.comparison_prompts:
### Prompts

- What is the purpose of each work?

---

- Key Difference: shorter political manifesto
- Second Difference: longer economic analysis
- Similarity: both critique capitalism

| Exile | Alienation |
| Manifesto | Class struggle |

materials.checklist:
## Checklist

☐ Complete plain-string table
☐ Verify separator prompts render cleanly
☐ Confirm generic headings are suppressed

content.expected_output: All edge-case materials rendered without markdown artefacts.

content.title: PEL orientation field showcase
content.activity_preamble: Before you open the materials, note how each block below should appear in order.
content.study_orientation: Work through this synthetic activity in one sitting (about 20 minutes). It exists only to validate renderer passthrough for self-study orientation fields.
content.intellectual_frame: You are checking layout like a learner scanning a page before starting tasks.
content.intellectual_coherence_bridge: Use what you noticed in the edge-case activity when judging whether orientation labels appear correctly here.
content.learner_task: Confirm Study orientation, Intellectual frame, and Connection labels all appear above this task block.

content.title: PEL reasoning field showcase
content.activity_preamble: This synthetic row validates Phase 2 reasoning field passthrough only.
content.reasoning_orientation: Trace mechanism and host interaction — do not memorise labels without evidence.
content.evidence_use_prompt: Quote the reference excerpt when you justify each row in your comparison table.
content.argument_structure_hint: For each claim: state the claim, cite one passage, then note one implication.
content.conceptual_contrast_prompt: Contrast positive-sense and negative-sense RNA viruses; avoid treating replication steps as interchangeable.
content.self_explanation_prompt: Before checking the model row, summarise your answer in one sentence.
content.learner_task: Confirm Disciplinary lens, How to think, Using evidence, Structuring your response, and Key distinction all appear above this task block.

content.title: Minimal activity row
content.learner_task: Confirm minimal rows still render a task block.

content.facilitator_actions: Time-check at 20 minutes; redirect to table if groups stall.
Prepare printed worksheets if using paper.

items.stem: Which HTML class wraps a single learning activity?
items.explanation: Each activity renders as an article.util-task-block.

items.statement: Pipe-only markdown tables should appear as literal pipe text in learner HTML.
items.explanation: Tables should render inside util-table-scroll wrappers.

items.stem: In one sentence, name one renderer path that normalises embedded list markers.
items.model_answer: Learning purpose outcomes or util-checkbox-list item text.

sections.heading: Study tips
sections.content:
- Re-read tables before submitting.
- Use headings in the browser print preview.
- Compare your answers with the checklist.

sections.heading: Facilitator support
sections.content:
## Run notes

- This page is **synthetic**; do not use as teaching content.
- Verify collapsed metadata in export footer.
- Session pacing: 95 minutes total.

limitations: Synthetic renderer stress fixture — not produced by Design Page workflow.
Does not define composition membership rules; see Sprint 25 contracts.
